# Code of Conduct

## Our commitment

AccessibleName is built on the belief that everyone deserves access to digital systems. That ethic applies to our community as much as to our software. We commit to making participation in this project a welcoming experience regardless of age, disability, ethnicity, gender identity, level of experience, nationality, race, religion, or sexual orientation.

## Expected behaviour

- Be kind and patient. Most of us are doing accessibility work in time stolen from other duties.
- Assume good faith. Ask questions before assigning blame.
- Centre the people who use assistive technology in design and review discussions. They are who we build for.
- Use accessible language and formatting in issues, PRs, and comments. (Yes, in this project we mean it literally.)
- Welcome newcomers. The accessibility field is small and we need more people in it.

## Unacceptable behaviour

- Personal attacks, harassment, or discriminatory language.
- Dismissing concerns from disabled contributors or users.
- Disclosure of others' private information without permission.
- Persistent bad-faith disruption after being asked to stop.

## Enforcement

If you experience or witness behaviour that violates this code, contact the maintainers at [accesslabs@cnib.ca]. Reports will be handled confidentially. Maintainers may take any action they deem appropriate, including warning, temporary ban, or permanent ban from the project.

## Attribution

This code adapts the [Contributor Covenant 2.1](https://www.contributor-covenant.org/version/2/1/code_of_conduct/) and is licensed under the [Creative Commons Attribution 4.0 International license](https://creativecommons.org/licenses/by/4.0/).
